example_summary: Iterates over slices of a string, yielding chunks of the specified size.
Takes an object and returns a list of (key, value) pairs. If the input is already a list of pairs, it is returned as-is.
Parses header link fields into a list of dictionaries, each containing attributes from a single link-value.
Converts platform-specific interval bounds to a consistent internal representation.
Checks whether the provided key is a boolean indexer, returning True if it is an array-like of booleans.
Ensures the input is returned as a pandas Index object, converting from a list or array if necessary.
Validates that an estimator has been fitted by checking for the presence of fitted attributes.
Turns a seed or RandomState instance into a numpy RandomState object suitable for reproducible random sampling.
Indexes into an array or dataframe using the given indices, supporting both integer and boolean indexing.
